free_text: 3He+3He reaction cross sections at 17.9, 21.7, and
24.0 MeV
Used at 17.9, 21.7, and 24.0 MeV.
Used at 17.9 and 24.0 MeV.
99.9% pure 3He gas at about 400
torr, contained in cylindrical cell with Kapton foil
window located at center of an evacuated scattering
chamber.
Telescope consisting of 3 silicon surface-
barrier detectors.
Also, E* method which depends on the
symmetry about 90 degrees in cm system of the
reactions for identical particles in the incident
channel.
Used to calibrate secondary
particle spectrum energies.)
Corrected for:
. contributions from low-energy tail of elastic peak,
. effect of collimator assembly on detector at forward
angles,
. 3He gas background.
updated to new data formalism, BIB updated.
BP: Updated entry, added doi, digitized
(unobtainable data) per an NRDC decision for data
sets that were published before 2000 (C32 of WP2022-
Taken as equal to alpha production cross section.
Production angular distribution data are extrapolated
to 0 degrees at forward angles and to at value of
zero from the largest measured angle. Data at 17.9
and 24.0 MeV result from combining data measured at
both facilities.
Error includes extrapolation uncertainties
added in quadrature to uncertainties due to counting
statistics, corrections and background subtractions.
Table II, page 391.
Calculated from production cross:
. at 24.0 MeV = d,
. below 24.0 MeV = 3He - 1/2(p - 2alpha - 3t - d)

The particle production cross sections
were obtained by integrating each particle spectrum
over energy and then integrating over solid angle.
Adopted errors. The largest source of
error, the 0 degree extrapolation, is not independent
for the two measurements, rather than combine the two
errors the smallest of the two was adopted.
Fig. 7, page 391.
